headline: Why MGM Resorts (MGM) Is Up 7.6% After Detailing Buybacks, BetMGM Payouts And Osaka Progress
summary: MGM Resorts International recently outlined a multi-year plan that includes aggressive annual share repurchases of around 7–8%, BetMGM’s first cash distributions after reaching profitability in 2025, and progress on projects such as the planned sale of Northfield Park, stabilization efforts on the Las Vegas Strip, and its Osaka integrated resort scheduled to open in 2030. At the same time, MGM Resorts and BetMGM have expanded their responsible gaming commitments with more than US$1.00...

headline: Dow Jones leads modest retreat as Wall Street aloof to Iran war
summary: 10am: Wall Street starts with modest falls Initial trading in New York was less pessimistic than in Europe, with losses for the main US indices considerably smaller in initial trades. The Dow Jones is the worst, down 0.65%, followed by the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq Composite both falling...

headline: 1 S&P 500 Stock with Impressive Fundamentals and 2 That Underwhelm
summary: The S&P 500 (^GSPC) is home to the biggest and most well-known companies in the market, making it a go-to index for investors seeking stability. But not all large-cap stocks are created equal - some are struggling with slowing growth, declining margins, or increased competition.

headline: MGM Resorts Highlights Responsible Gaming Push And Sports Ties For Investors
summary: MGM Resorts International (NYSE:MGM) and BetMGM announced a $1 million commitment to responsible gaming, including a three year research project into sports wagering impacts and expanded safeguards across their platforms. The initiative was launched during Problem Gambling Awareness Month, aligning product changes and research funding with a period of heightened focus on gambling related risks. MGM Resorts is also set to co host "She Got Game" in Las Vegas, an event honoring U.S. women’s...
